title: Tapping to Commit to Growth and Giving
subtitle: A tapping practice for choosing continued expansion without pressure, and anchoring the link between wealth and generosity
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 83 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the temptation to stop once enough is achieved, and moves toward a commitment to keep growing because growth keeps giving.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 83 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel satisfied with what you have built but uncertain whether there is a reason to keep growing, or when the drive to expand has quietened.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 83 of a 12-week money programme. Week 12 theme: live abundance as normal. Day 83 addresses the quiet temptation to stop once enough is built, and works toward a sustainable commitment to growth rooted in generosity rather than pressure.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I've already created so much, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I could just stop here, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I might wonder if I need to grow more, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I choose to keep expanding in joy.
Side of eye: growth can stay fun and light.
Under eye: giving can keep pace with growth.
Under nose: I love supporting projects and people.
Chin: I enjoy seeing money do good.
Collarbone: I welcome the next level with ease.
Under arm: I commit to growth that serves all.
Top of head: I keep creating abundance and impact.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I grow wealth as a way to give more.
Side of eye: I let expansion feel natural and exciting.
Under eye: I love watching generosity rise with income.
Under nose: I keep investing in people and dreams.
Chin: I trust wealth to fuel good in the world.
Collarbone: I commit to a lifetime of growth and giving.
Under arm: I celebrate abundance that benefits everyone.
Top of head: I stay open to ever-expanding prosperity.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 83 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
